pages.text: Madame clerk yes this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings Act chapter 231 public law 1975 the annual notice was emailed to the star-ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on October 31st 2019 and published in the West Orange Chronicle on November 7th 2019 councilman Garino president councilman crackovia Councilwoman tooth Brown Councilwoman McCartney council president Kathleen o present good evening welcome West Orange to our February 4th meeting so already another month of 2020 has gone by we're going to start tonight's presentations we have two guest presentations here this evening and tonight and we're going to start with having our volunteers from the National Council of Jewish women's group coming down and cjw making presentation for the national teen dating abuse awareness and Prevention Month so I'm going to ask Sharon Falken Melinda Udo and Rita Marshall to please come down as we make this presentation thank you ladies right ove

r here first of all thank you very much for coming down you're very active active volunteer group giving your time to women in our community especially with such a topic of teen dating abuse and you've been working on this aware project for many years so I'm going to read the proclamation and then I'm going to ask you to tell a little bit about the organization and how residents in town can volunteer their time and how they can get involved and all three ladies are part of our community they're all residents of West Orange so we thank you for all their service in advance proclamation for mayor precedes the Parisi whereas one in three teens in a dating relationship experienced some form of abuse physical sexual emotional or verbal whereas females between 16 to 24 have the highest rate of intimate partner violence and whereas nearly 1.5 million high school students nationwide experience physical abuse from a dating partner every year the teen dating abuse awareness project addresses the issue of teen dating abuse with high school students parents and educators t

he teen dating abuse awareness project seeks to educate about abuse and dating relationships by discussing the warning signs how to break up safety the role of friends and what constitutes a healthy relationship now therefore be it resolved that I Robert D Parisi marathons with West Orange New Jersey to hereby proclaim February 2020 as national teen dating violence awareness and Prevention Month in the township of West Orange so thank you ladies and if you could give us an overall those are some very strong thank you those are very some strong facts that were stated in this proclamation good evening my name is Melinda Udell and I am chairperson of the teen dating abuse awareness program which is part of National Council for Jewish women in Essex County and I'm here tonight with other members of our program as was stated before t da a is invited into high schools throughout the school year throughout Essex County to educate students on healthy relationships and to teach them the warning signs of verbal emotional physical and sex

ual abuse one out of three women younger women and one out of four men young men will find themselves in an abusive relationship by the time they are out of their 20s so abuse is an issue for all communities February is national teen dating violence prevention and awareness month and we are so thankful and appreciate this proclamation from the town of West Orange in recognition of the work that we do so thank you so much here with my colleagues and thank you thank you save what day ladies thank you very much your work and you've been working with high schools throughout the area and very important the statistics were overwhelming and you know having children myself and working with so many with with the school district for many years your volunteer services are invaluable to the community so thank you for all that you do for West Orange and the surrounding communities next I like to call up Camille Lorenzo so this to the podium to make a presentation on Pleasant Valley productions this year we had a wonderful se

ason with with with your production team of opening reopening oz pack and providing great entertainment to our community very family oriented programming I know just from talk in the community a lot of great events people have a good time and but you know we're here to go over the report and the events and to see how well you did and to see what more I like for you also as you present to us also let us know what we can do for you I say community as well thank you so I that's a nice picture so I had 75 photos you knew I was going you know what say you knew I would say it right Joe you had to know I was gonna say it I had 75 photos great photos of this past summer some people in this room were in the photos but mostly it was really great music events and something happened to the file when I sent it to Joe so there's only 18 so enjoy and I really apologize that I have five pages of narrative and only eighteen photos so why have plenty of photos maybe next time we'll see all the pictures or you can visit the website to see all the 144

great pictures so we're here to give our report before I start I would like to thank everybody on the Township Council for trusting us eight months ago to do a good job I think we did I think we did a good job good evening on behalf of Pleasant Valley Productions and our board of trustees and our mighty volunteers I am happy to report on our progress at AHS pack these last eight months please enjoy the slideshow of not 73 photos taken from our first season they are not in any particular order but pay attention and you may see yourself in a photo or two or maybe not almost immediately following our agreement with the township last May we plowed into the work at AHS back when I used the word we I am referring to a group of volunteers known as PvP our initial goal was to prepare the facility for a summer of community events we reached out for help utilizing many of our resources and relationships we have established over our years experience in the performing arts to create a season of entertainmen

t we exhibited at the Western Street Fair in an effort to engage the community and let them know of our plans for a spark the first event Lattin night was produced by the West our Hispanic Association the event featured live music by Ruben's a DJ and beautiful cultural dancing and food next up was our summer opener called oz pack Summerfest which provided five hours of family activities including the Bella princesses the mad science show martial arts exhibits showcasing our local talent face-painting bouncy house food trucks a beer and wine garden followed by our feature performance New York state of mind the music of Billy Joel the band was sponsored by advantage pest control and we would like to publicly thank them once again for their support our proposed date for Summerfest this year is May 30th the following weekend we produced an incredible evening of music Broadway under the stars in partnership with the Encore Orchestra of New Jersey we had seven singers from Broadway and regional performanc

es accompanied by an outstanding thirty piece Orchestra this event received rave reviews and it is our hope that we will continue to present Broadway under the stars again this coming season the very next week we hosted a West Orange Recreation Department concert event the Tom Petty tribute band refugee performed to an audience of strong music lovers as it was a hundred and two degrees that day but for some reason the evening breeze at oz pack seemed to remedy the humidity and it was a great night with no rain the rec department has already booked their concert for 2020 so mark your calendars for July 24th as we bring you Ring of Fire the music of Johnny Cash before I move on to August I would like to report that none of what we have so far accomplished at aspect would have been possible without the support of our community partners and West Orange volunteers I mean physical support on the field at our events lugging tents moving chairs loaning tables selling tickets pouring beverages emptying garbage commingling recycling and so mu

ch more so thank you to everyone especially Meg Ric Joe and all of our PvP volunteers wearing the green staff shirts all summer long thank you we were very busy in August presenting our first full production at oz back hair the tribal rock musical we ran the show for four consecutive nights and got very lucky with no rainout performances everyone loved it and audience members made a point of saying how very cool it was to see such a professional style show at an outdoor venue with food trucks and beverages one person said it's like Broadway but in the park we would like to thank Columbia Bank in the West Orange rec department for your support of our community theater production we are super excited to announce our 2020 production title as soon as licensing is confirmed so please stay tuned for more information and audition dates the West Orange Community Band concert was scheduled for August 21st but unfortunately was moved to Liberty middle school due to rain it was a great night of music performed by West Orange Talent the West Orange Community 257 00:14:35,279 --

> 00:14:39,240 Band with an opening set from our very own jazzy lady Lynette shared accompanied by West Orange resident Fran testa our final performance and August featured the professional dance company free space dance from Montclair showing the final performance of their summer program we have plans to bring them back in August for two nights of dance performance and we encourage you to come out and experience the free space dance talent September was amazing as we hosted one of our favorite events West Orange Idol the downtown West Orange Alliance sponsors a Talent Competition for West Orange residents of all ages to showcase their voices and receive valuable feedback from the selected judges of the competition we had more than 20 contestants and expect an even greater turnout when we host this event for downtown again this year on Sunday June 28th look out for entry details coming soon the following week we co-produced a music event called Carl brister's love and unity festival the lineup of talented artists was terrific and it featured the music of West Orange

resident Carl Brister we closed out our season with a fun-filled Fall Festival which was co-produced by the downtown West Orange Alliance and featured field games arcade games pumpkin races a pie binky contest DJ music food provided by the West Orange Rotary Club and a lot of very happy faces and the many children came out to enjoy the day soon after that the outdoor lights and lighting stress were struck and stored inside for the winter and it got us thinking about our offseason programming with a group of talented performing artists it seems such a waste to take the winter off although I personally wouldn't mind because I am so very tired but we we are producers of Performing Arts and we did promise to provide so we will present an indoor performance of a very small but familiar title play called Driving Miss Daisy some of you may remember the movie with Jessica Tandy and Morgan Freeman if you've been hiding some talent and you are a seasoned actor please consider coming out to audition on February 10th

:57,660 and 11th there is limited seating for this performance but we will present it two consecutive weekends in April the following weekend we plan to present a brand new musical piece written as theatre for young audiences it will be presented as a workshop staged reading and we will have special invitations for elementary age students new to us pack this coming summer will be our teen workshop company production of peterpan junior this production will be open to ages 12 to 18 our team production and our protege program are part of pvp's long time commitment to educating young performers and fostering interest in the backstage element of the performing arts for more information on our protege program you may visit our website so many of you have reached out to talk about the cool things happening at AHS pack actually today I got an email from someone at the point just to mention that the townhouse community up at the point is thrilled with the new programming a toss back I just wanted to say that however they reached out and asked for us to have a community-wide

pages.text: welcome west orange to our tuesday june 23rd west orange town council virtual meeting we welcome everyone back for this evening and madame clerk i'll let you make the announcement thank you this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings act chapter 231 public law 1975. the annual notice was emailed to the star ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on october 31st 2019 and published in the west orange chronicle on november 7 2019 councilman guarino president councilman kirkoviak councilwoman brown councilwoman mccartney president council president casolino president you matt thank you madam clerk um unfortunately as we been starting out the council meeting for the past few months is to take a moment to remember um our residents that have passed from the copa virus i believe today's count was 171. so we lost a few more residents since the last ti

me we met so we just paused for a moment of silence for them and those all are still sick and suffering from the disease thank you and as we open up it's important to remember that you know we are still in covet phase we're in phase two we'll talk about opening up in a little bit but please everyone please please remember to wear your mask and to practice social distancing when out in public tonight we're going to change things up a little bit we wanted to uh bring on um some important important member of our youth community uh salaam um i'm sorry my glasses um islam wall day uh from the youth caucus group that has just formed here in west orange i met salaam the other evening at colgate park our youth in town very imp very impressive organized celebration of juneteenth uh within a week within a week's time and it was great we have not had a celebration here in township um it's been a holiday for many since 1866 65 00:02:38,319 --

> 00:02:43,120 and i thought it was important um that we educate our public the past month we've had some unrest in our nation and a lot of our young voices have been heard and i think it's important that we take the time and we listen uh the other evening i was very impressed the uh perry bashkoff from town uh and also uh works for us uh he did this on his own he had a few of our youth leaders in the township on his show and that's i had i was glad i sought to seek out salaam the other night when i met her at colgate park because i wanted i thought it was important that how the our youth have been articulating their voices so well and i wanted to bring her on and introduce her uh to you and let her speak for a few minutes on the importance of this celebration what it meant for our community and also what the black lives matter movement means to them as well so salaam welcome this evening hi thank you for having me thank you you did a great job the other night yo

u had beautiful weather a great crowd of i guess over 200 people attended the event i think so yeah right right a lot and i think it was just great that um just people were able to relax a little bit get get out um everybody was great it was a great area because there was plenty of room to uh social distance and and still be able to um you know see one another so why did you wanted to organize the event for the community this year um it definitely was not just me by myself it was on the entire western youth caucus we were working together and we wanted to um have a celebration for many reasons one of the main ones was because we were just like on a phone call and we were handling some paperwork that we had to do and um uh jordan's joining us scott young she was um had a great idea saying how we need something to bring this town together in unity and because especially with the black community with the recent news that's been going on it's been making help making us be

very hopeless and we wanted something to like bring everyone together kind of celebrate juneteenth and the juneteenth is an extremely important day because although the emancipation proclamation was issued in 1862 all slaves were not um all enslaved people were not free until 1865 with texas being the last state and that's the day that we celebrate with juneteenth so i thought it was important to celebrate the holiday one for because one we wanted the town to come together and have something to celebrate and two it's not a very well-known holiday and needs to become very well known because rather than celebrating independence day on july 4th we we should celebrate juneteenth because but when july 4th independence day all americans weren't even free at that point with juneteenth that celebrates the last the day that all enslaved people were free and not many people this isn't taught in um our education system i know that like i had to lunch on my own so i wanted to reach out we wanted to reach out and to

,680 teach also children them portions of this day i think it's amazing when you think back to that time how slow the information traveled and here we are almost 200 years later and how quick the information travels with social media and how you can get the word out so quickly so it it it's it's amazing that it took that long for the work the uh word to travel and lauren to go the next slide and so nelson mandela had said education is the most powerful weapon you can use to change the world and i believe that's uh where a lot of voices are speaking out now so if you'd like to share with us as well uh the the importance of the blacks lives matter movement to you and your youth caucus group yeah um definitely so the black lives matter movement is imperative to intertwine into our daily lives black lives matter is not a trend it's a movement to increase solidarity with the black community and raise awareness on the injustices that black people face in this nation and also across the globe and although

it is commonly associated with police police brutality the vastness of the movement doesn't just stop right there black lives matter includes health care the racial disparities and health care racism redlining mass incarceration and that's just to name a few and um however we need to uh intertwine in our daily lives because although we have we might feel as if we don't have little or no impact on a macro level meaning like we can't we probably can't change the systemic racism we can on a micro level we can definitely try to change this town from the anti-black ordinances in this town and policies in the school district to anti-black education curriculum to microaggressions that are perpetuated by a lot of people within this town we need to not acknowledge the black lives matter movement in our daily lives as we don't even realize the weight of our impacts within the town and the weight of our words and it's just that for anyone who doesn't really agree with black lives matter is not really something to agree

pages.text: thank you welcome West Orange to the Tuesday August 11 2020 Township council meeting Madam clerk this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with Section Five of the open public meetings act chapter 231 Public Law 1975 the annual notice was emailed to The Star Ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on October 31st 20 9 and published in the West Orange Chronicle on November 7th 2019 councilman Gino pres councilman kovak present councilwoman Mato Brown councilwoman McCartney present council president castelino present thank you Madame Clerk and welcome everyone it's been about a month since we last met so we hope everyone is doing well we hope everyone uh survived last week's storm uh 2020 uh still uh packing some punches and uh last week was no different so we like to thank everyone on our OEM uh teams our police and firefighters uh our Public Works uh for all being out there and our C team for supporting our Township we had charging 31 00:01:25,079

stations last week so the ser and OEM have provided so we we want to thank all for uh pitching in once again for another Township emergency uh but as usual we all came together uh starting off the program this evening um you know it's it's been a month um the infection rate has gone way down uh we're doing great everyone keep wearing their mask um we had uh two two folks passed with covid since then um which we're we're sorry to hear and we're looking forward to someday to do a townwide celebration for all those uh who we have lost and unfortunately um I just want to recognize and it wasn't due to co but we had a longtime public defender for a town uh Township and our uh parliamentarian for our Democratic committee uh a dedicated public servant Jonathan Goodman who who passed this morning so I just want to give a moment of silence for all those who I just previously mentioned thank you and we extend our uh our condolences to the Goodman family um so gonna um we have a surprise today we have a group of our talented uh actually Rising uh senior

Junior and our graduates few graduates here this evening from the nille bani Foundation Advisory Board and I'm going to turn the presentation over to councilman uh garina who invited them on this evening so councilman if you would like to uh introduce our guests sure thank you council president and good evening to everyone um i' like to you know welcome of course and thank Darlene Ane Ana Brett and Nicole sorry Nicole couldn't be here tonight but to thank them for adding real excitement and uh Ingenuity to The Pedestrian safety advisor board they have been a tremendous asset uh they've they've really worked with us they've been allow you know enabled to work with you know such great Traffic Engineers and Community residents who are really big in pedestrian safety particularly bike safety walk you know stay fruits to school and and all our other programs for complete street so we're extremely proud of them they should be very proud of themselves they really came through and every assignment they they approached they approached it in

,239 150% and I I hope we have them come back the ones who and even the ones who have graduated we hope you'll do come back and we hope you'll encourage other youth from the high school to participate and be involved so not to talk too much uh council president I'd like to turn over the presentation to the student Advisory Board and thank you thank you coun okay Madam moderator if you could put the first slide on great good evening everyone I first want to say that I hope we're finding you in good health and spirits and I hope that you're enjoying your summer during these extremely difficult times um I also want to thank council president celino and the council members for giving us the opportunity to present today next slide my name is Darlene FIS and I have been a part of the nikil bad Lani Foundation youth Advisory Board since its creation our goal is to empower our youth to make a difference in our community by allowing students to express their ideas and develop traffic recommendation projects together we place our youth at the Forefront

of saving lives preventing crashes and injuries and educating others about the dangers of reckless driving specifically we generate ideas for productive change we develop specific Traffic Safety Improvement plans we work with the n bani Foundation the township and other relevant organizations and we Implement and finalize our versions of our plans next slide our board is made up of three committees public policy education and Municipal each committee is centered around different aspects of traffic safety yet all committed to making our streets safer our public policy committee advocates for traffic bills to become laws by working with individuals like our assembly men our education committee educates our peers within the high school the middle school and the community at large and our Municipal committee implements Traffic Safety Improvement plans with our community using the board and other relevant organizations next slide hi everybody my name is Brett zon uh the goal of the public policy 146 00:0

committee is to advocate for bills to become laws so one bill that the youth Advisory board has lobbied for in the last couple years has been Michelle Souza's Bill Michelle was only 17 when she was struck by a car while crossing a street her family was devastated by her loss made even harder because they didn't know the cause for the crash and there was no closure for the family because no drug test was performed on the driver who caused the crash this bill mandates that drug and alcohol testing be performed by obtaining a blood sample when there's a fatality in the car crash over the year we've collected more than 200 signatures for the bill additionally we traveled to tr to meet with assemblymen Wimberly and mcken primary sponsors of the Michelle sues Bill to discuss the status of the legislation and how it could be accelerated through the state house hi everyone my name is an and I am a rising Junior at the high school and another bill that we have decided to advocate for is Bill number 1354 the bill requires permit holders to 174 00:07:

record a minimum of 50 hours of practice driving of which at least 10 hours should be in darkness currently there is no requirement of hours in place it also extends the time the permit holder is required to get a probationary license from six months to 12 months this allows the driver to gain more experience New Jersey is one of the three states that is yet to pass the bill under the gdl system we hope to be able to pass it in the upcoming legislative season next slide please hi everyone my name is Ana and I've been involved in the youth advisory board's policy committee to help bring change in state policy in December of 2019 we headed over to the state house in Trenton to learn more about the lawmaking process we got to Tour the State House learn more about the history of each branch and even look at the House and Senate chambers in addition we met assemblyman Benson assemblyman mcken assemblyman Wimberly and assemblywoman Mila JC we discussed the bills that the youth Advi

sory Board wanted to help push forward and learned what we could do as our next steps in the early months of 2020 we continued keeping contact with the assemblymen and hope to continue pushing for these bills to be passed next slide as part of the education committee we helped promote Traffic Safety awareness by visiting Liberty and Rosevelt Middle School presenting to over 700 students we tailored our presentation to deal with relevant safety issues which were primarily pedestrian and bus safety through an engaging presentation and activities calling for audience participation we helped teach and reinforce some vital guidelines at the end of our presentation all the middle schoolers Took The Pedestrian safety pledge by placing their thumbprint on our pledge poster we continue to spread awareness at West Orange High School through traffic safety week since high schoolers are already beginning to learn to drive we covered a wider range of topics for each day of the week these included the it could weit texting and driving stand 232 0

driving Under the Influence virtual simulation I don not drink and drive trivia writing to local assemblymen for a letter campaign and pedestrian safety trivia along with the activities there were quiz questions and prizes given out for correct answers at both schools we sent our pre out we sent out pre and post surveys to measure if our presentations and activities were successful at help and helpful to educate our fellow students we were pleased to find a considerable increase in the awareness the students exhibited and want to continue educating our district by targeting a wider audience and having more involved activities next slide the municipality committee worked with the West Orange pedestrian safety Advisory Board to make recommendations to improve the safety on West Orange streets last school year we had raised a pedestrian safety concern on Pleasant Valley Way in Woodland Avenue in March a pedestrian push to walk button was installed as a result of our recommendation we also conducted a 261 00

lighting assessment on Pleasant Valley way to evalu evaluate an inadequate Lighting on the street our findings showed that there were eight locations where the lighting was inadequate we also analyzed West Orange crash data to pinpoint the streets that had reckless driving activity we hope to continue working with The Pedestrian safety board next slide please last year we organized and managed the painting of a permanent stop for nille safety pledge mural in West Orange High School the goal of this project was to reinforce good driving behavior and spread awareness of distracted driving Additionally the completion of the project led to changes in the driver's education curriculum at West Orange High School now each Soph each sophomore who passes their written driver's test is required to take the Pledge on the mural and put their thumb print on the painting in January this project was recognized as a top 10 project in New Jersey by the New Jersey Association of student councils we received this award at an annual

pages.text: welcome west orange to our tuesday september 22nd 2020 uh virtual township council meeting adam clerk this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings act chapter 231 public law 1975. the annual notice was emailed to the star ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on october 31st 2019 and published in the west orange chronicle on november 7th 2019. councilman garino president councilman kocoviak president councilwoman too brown councilwoman mccartney president council president casolino present thank you madam clerk and welcome everyone i hope everybody has been safe and well i'm very happy to report since our last meeting we're doing very well with uh covert cases there hasn't been any additional debts due to the virus in the township very few um uh positive cases and we're really happy to hear that so keep continuing to wear your mask as you 33

frequent public venues or restaurants um have been getting a pretty good participation they've been having some indoor dining uh 20 capacity also our fitness centers uh have reopened up and we're glad to see uh folks going back to their normal daily routine safely so we hope uh everyone could be supportive of all our small business in the township tonight we don't have a special guest on so i've asked our because i wanted to have our township clerk ms carnaval speak to the public there are a lot of concerns about the vote by mail we didn't have all the information the last meeting we do have uh information this meeting and madam clerk is going to walk us through she has a few slides for us on what to expect you will be expecting ballots soon in the mail and i'm going to turn the program over to her so she could educate us all thank you madam clerk you're welcome um yeah we've been getting a lot of uh phone calls

pages.text: welcome west orange to our tuesday december 15 2020 uh township town council meeting madam clerk this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings act chapter 231 public law 1975. the annual notice was emailed to the star ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on october 31st 2019 and published in the west orange chronicle on november 7th 2019. put in the wash a long time ago councilman guarino present councilman kokoviak uh president mr lapore oh yeah councilwoman matthew brown president councilwoman mccartney president council president casolino present thank you madam clerk and again good evening west orange tonight we're going to start the meeting go off we're starting a little earlier tonight at 6 p.m so we want to thank everyone who joined us early we have a full action package ended this evening so we wanted to get a

little early start we're going to start off with a presentation from councilman garina who has been our pedestrian safety liaison for the western pedestrian safety commission and we have a few special guests this evening they uh the commission has been working really hard on keeping our streets safe in the township so i like to introduce councilman garina to welcome our guest thank you council president it's a pleasure to be here tonight and to thank all the members that i see from my west orange pedestrian safety board i'll also like to thank dr cascone for being here uh he's been a great supporter of our programs um you know scott smarsh and the west orange traffic enforcement bureau thank you scott we couldn't do a lot of things without you um like to thank will uh zurib uh zach alley and lisa lee from easy ride and to thank you uh for supporting west orange and making west orange be the premier uh municipality for pedestrian

safety street smart and uh for all the projects that you've assisted us with and taking us forward to being one of the top ranking municipalities when it comes to pedestrian safety um like i said seven years ago when i created the board it was on a personal thing and meeting with other personal people who've had issues with traffic accident you know traffic crashes personal injury myself almost losing both my legs by hitting being hit by a car uh raw's i know you won't mind me saying but then god your father's soul when your dad was killed on pleasant valley way it's when you take people's personal tragedies and you merge them into a program of trying to make the community a safer community for our motorists and of course for our pedestrians so um you know it it just means a lot and i'm very very proud of my board uh i'm not going away i'm still going to be very involved with street you know with our pedestrian safety board 98 00:

and i would just like to move on and not hold everybody up and just introduce you to our street smart campaign our campaign kickoff for 2020 and 2021 so uh thank you madam president all right well i take it from there okay if we could go down one slide thank you very much so my name is will yarzab first of all i want to thank the mayor the township township council west orange police department and easy ride for organizing this campaign it takes a lot of people to do this and there certainly has been a great interest from west orange and with jerry's leadership my hat is off to him so i work for the north jersey transportation planning authority and my job is to coordinate street smart new jersey pedestrian safety campaigns we started the program back in 2013 and since then we've partnered with more than 165 communities across new jersey although the north jersey transportation is the metropolitan planning organization for the 13 northern counties the street smart 128 00:04

new jersey program covers the entire state more communities we get to work with the more we can help change the behaviors that contribute to these pedestrian crashes unfortunately already this year 550 people have been killed in car crashes and that includes 160 pedestrians and 20 cyclists so we are continuing to work with the different partners to reduce these numbers we have seen great success in communities that do a street smart campaign including war drivers are stopping for people crossing and more people are using crosswalks and crossing properly so i look forward to a successful campaign here in west orange now for safety safety is a top priority at north jersey transportation planning authority in addition to coordinating the street smart we also fund projects through the local safety program last month our board approved our board of trustees approved 26.4 million dollars in federal grants 160 00:0

to help make safety improvements in 39 intersections in essex county that includes two intersections in west orange washington street at wachong ave and pleasant valley way at woodland half improvements will include updated signals and crosswalks ada compliant curb ramps pavement markings and adjustments to the traffic signal phasing to make it safer for pedestrians to cross now infrastructure improvements are fantastic and they will help make our roads safer but so do public education campaigns like the one you're undertaking so again the street smart new jersey program which uses education outreach and enforcement to change those unsafe behaviors from both drivers and pedestrians and we want to use this program to eliminate those crashes during the campaign you'll see signs literature a video educational programs police officers out about encouraging the program and explaining the program 192 00:06:43,759

we also use repetitive messaging to ensure drivers and pedestrians know and understand the law it's important that we all are safe and share the roadways so we do a lot of social media you'll see it across the social media platform and we really want people to totally understand the law although the law has changed too drivers must stop for pedestrians they have to have sufficient time to stop so on both sides of the coin here we need people to cooperate so by combining the public education and enforcement we can change these behaviors it contributes to the total crashes and together we can make a difference so remember drive smart walk smart and be street smart thank you very much thank you will and i believe officer smarts is going to go uh next actually is dr cascone dr cascone thank you well good evening everyone um council president castellin i appreciate the opportunity to be here members of the town council thank you
